$comment: c50 (D-CSUBSET-ARRAY-DECAY-TO-VOID-PTR): an ARRAY or string literal passed to a void* parameter (memcpy/memset dst/src) decays to a pointer-to-element (C 6.3.2.1p3) then converts to void* (C 6.3.2.3p1). Frontier: sqlite sqlite3VXPrintf `memcpy(buf, "-Inf", 5)` (buf is char[N]) -> S0003 on both buf (array) and "-Inf" (string literal). ROOT: the isAssignable array-decay arm (type_rules.hpp) only admitted Array<T>->Ptr<T> (same element); Array<char>->Ptr<void> fell through, and the Ptr->void arm never fired (rhs is Array not Ptr) -> so char*/u8* pointer args passed but bare ARRAY names + string literals failed. FIX (NOT §B; one disjunct per tier reusing the existing implicitToVoidPtr flag): semantic isAssignable + HIR coerce both admit Array->Ptr<void> + emit the synthetic Array->Ptr Cast (MIR re-types by the void* target, element-agnostic; NO MIR/verifier change). a[8]: memset(a,0,8) then memcpy(a,"xyz",4) -> a={'x','y','z',0,...} -> exit 42. RED-ON-DISABLE: revert either arm -> memcpy(a,"xyz",4) -> S0003. memcpy/memset link against real libc on all legs (as ffi_memcpy proves). Scope: clears the array/string-literal->void* family (buf, "-Inf", "unistr('", probably x); the function-designator chacha20_init->void* is a SEPARATE frontier (FnSig->Ptr<void>).
